pattern: Mapping each letter to its keyboard row and keeping only words whose letters all share one row filters the list with a simple set membership check.
hints: Assign every letter a row number based on the three keyboard rows, ignoring letter case.
A word qualifies only if all of its letters map to the same row.
Compare each word's set of row numbers; keep it when that set has exactly one element, preserving input order.

stress.description: random mix of single-row and multi-row words with varied case
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = ['qwertyuiop', 'asdfghjkl', 'zxcvbnm']
    n = max(1, min(size, 20))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            row = rng.choice(rows)
            length = rng.randint(1, 6)
            w = ''.join(rng.choice(row) for _ in range(length))
        else:
            length = rng.randint(2, 6)
            w = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(length))
        w = ''.join(ch.upper() if rng.random() < 0.3 else ch for ch in w)
        words.append(w)
    return [words]
